name: International Aid Charity
mission: International Aid Charity (IAC) provides emergency and ongoing aid relief, education, orphan sponsorship and social support services to marginalized peoples throughout the world. Our donors and volunteers work directly with communities to offer both physical and emotional health through education, housing and vocational training for orphans and widows, sports and creative arts programs. IAC offers a foundation of hope and resources upon which those in need can build and thrive.
theoryOfChange: Website states it provides schooling, health care, emotional support for widows and orphans and vocational training to empower people to build their own future, and offers a foundation of hope and resources upon which those in need can build and thrive.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to fulfill zakat obligations through a lean, highly efficient humanitarian organization serving high-conflict zones.
donor_motivations: Want to ensure nearly 90% of their gift goes directly to programs
Prioritize aid for refugees and orphans in the Middle East
Value organizations with low overhead and zero fundraising costs
Seek to fulfill zakat through verified asnaf categories
giving_considerations: The organization is currently navigating a period of revenue contraction
Governance practices are still maturing to meet all BBB standards
Focus is on direct aid rather than long-term systemic advocacy
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking a charity with a massive global footprint or those who require 100% compliance with all BBB governance standards.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade B reflects verified outcome tracking and a documented theory of change, though third-party evaluations are limited.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: IAC provides immediate relief (food, medical) and long-term support (education, orphan care) to break the cycle of poverty in displaced communities.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that direct aid like food drives and soccer programs effectively improves beneficiary well-being.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: Recent revenue declines suggest the organization has significant capacity to absorb new funding to restore program levels.

strengths.detail: The charity directs <cite id="1">87.8% of its budget to program services</cite>, ensuring donor funds reach beneficiaries directly.
IAC maintains a <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, maximizing the impact of every donation.
The organization holds <cite id="1">4.4 months of operating reserves</cite>, which is well above the industry standard for humanitarian groups.

strengths_deep_dive: International Aid Charity excels in financial stewardship and direct impact. Their <cite id="1">perfect 100/100 scores in Culture and Leadership</cite> from Charity Navigator highlight a stable internal governance structure. The organization's ability to deliver aid at a cost of <cite id="3">$49.53 per beneficiary</cite> is considered exceptional for the humanitarian sector. This efficiency is bolstered by a <cite id="1">Gold Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, indicating that the charity proactively shares its goals and strategies with the public.
rich_narrative.summary: International Aid Charity (IAC) provides critical humanitarian relief and social support to marginalized communities across the Middle East and South Asia. The organization maintains <cite id="1">an overall score of 87.87.5/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting a strong commitment to accountability and financial health. IAC focuses on direct service delivery, including <cite id="2">orphan sponsorship, refugee schools, and emergency relief</cite> in high-conflict zones like Gaza and Syria. While the organization reported <cite id="3">$1,481,959 in total revenue for 2023</cite>, it experienced a significant <cite id="6">revenue decline of 43% in the most recent fiscal year</cite>. Despite this volatility, IAC remains highly efficient in its resource allocation. The charity directs <cite id="1">87.8% of its total expenses toward programs</cite>, which is significantly higher than the <cite id="3">87.8% program ratio reported in some tax filings</cite>, suggesting a lean operational model. Furthermore, IAC demonstrates <cite id="1">exceptional fundraising efficiency</cite>, spending $0.00 to raise every $1 in public support. This financial discipline allows the charity to maintain <cite id="1">4.4 months of working capital</cite>, providing a resilient buffer against economic instability.
